evidence_boundary: Source-level public comparison. URLs and product capabilities should be live-verified before installation, trading, or public superiority claims.
comparison_goal: Show why a single Stark finance/trading router is useful even when many excellent vendor MCPs, skills, APIs, and trading frameworks already exist.

candidates.name: Alpaca MCP
candidates.route_role: US equities, options, crypto snapshots, paper trading, and broker execution drafts.
candidates.what_stark_must_do_better: Gate live actions, separate data lookup from trade execution, and label paper versus live environments.

candidates.name: OpenBB Docs MCP / OpenBB
candidates.route_role: TradFi, macro, equity, ETF, and crypto research workbench plus OpenBB documentation routing.
candidates.what_stark_must_do_better: Provide lighter natural-language routing across Web3, TradFi, and execution safety.

candidates.name: QuantConnect MCP / LEAN
candidates.route_role: Strategy research, reproducible backtests, paper deployment, and live algorithm handoff.
candidates.what_stark_must_do_better: Force assumptions, fees, slippage, drawdown, out-of-sample checks, and live approval boundaries.

candidates.name: Alpha Vantage MCP
candidates.route_role: Low-friction market data fallback and indicator source.
candidates.what_stark_must_do_better: Cross-check delayed or rate-limited data and avoid turning indicators into recommendations.

candidates.name: Financial Modeling Prep MCP
candidates.route_role: Equity due diligence, comps, valuation inputs, and fundamentals.
candidates.what_stark_must_do_better: Tie fundamentals to timestamps, filings, and market-data caveats.

candidates.name: FactSet MCP
candidates.route_role: Institutional-grade TradFi data route when entitlement and redistribution constraints are known.
candidates.what_stark_must_do_better: Label entitlement, source definitions, timestamp, and redistribution limits before using institutional data in public artifacts.

candidates.name: Twelve Data MCP
candidates.route_role: Multi-asset quotes and technical data cross-check.
candidates.what_stark_must_do_better: Mark venue, delay status, and whether a feed is decision-grade.

candidates.name: Unusual Whales MCP
candidates.route_role: Options-flow and market-structure signal source.
candidates.what_stark_must_do_better: Treat flow as signal evidence, not a trade recommendation, and require quote/liquidity confirmation.

candidates.name: Massive / Polygon.io MCP
candidates.route_role: High-quality data feed candidate for snapshots, bars, and news.
candidates.what_stark_must_do_better: Route by data need and explain subscription/data-gap limits.

candidates.name: Tradier MCP
candidates.route_role: Options quotes, contract checks, paper orders, and broker execution drafts.
candidates.what_stark_must_do_better: Demand order previews, max-loss checks, and explicit confirmation before live submission.

candidates.name: Robinhood Agentic Trading
candidates.route_role: Agentic brokerage surface candidate only when the user has explicitly configured limits and account scope.
candidates.what_stark_must_do_better: Treat setup and trading as high-risk live execution; require account, budget, max-loss, and confirmation gates.

candidates.name: cTrader MCP servers
candidates.route_role: FX/CFD broker-platform route for account review, chart analysis, demo trading, and gated live actions.
candidates.what_stark_must_do_better: Preserve broker-specific risk assumptions, lot sizing, margin, stop-out, and explicit demo/live separation.

candidates.name: Binance Skills Hub
candidates.route_role: Crypto market data, token research, account reads, and execution-capable Binance surfaces.
candidates.what_stark_must_do_better: Keep Binance as an internal route, not the user-facing mental model; gate every state-changing command.

candidates.name: Dune MCP
candidates.route_role: Historical onchain metrics, cohort analysis, token issuance, holder and transfer studies.
candidates.what_stark_must_do_better: Validate table semantics, bound queries, and cross-check with chain/explorer truth.

candidates.name: Alchemy MCP
candidates.route_role: Wallet, token, NFT, transaction, and simulation-adjacent chain checks.
candidates.what_stark_must_do_better: Keep reads separate from signing and select the correct app/network before queries.

candidates.name: Etherscan MCP
candidates.route_role: Explorer truth for verified source, logs, transactions, and contract facts.
candidates.what_stark_must_do_better: Use explorer data as evidence, not as final risk judgment without behavior checks.

candidates.name: Coinbase CDP / AgentKit MCP
candidates.route_role: Coinbase developer stack route for Web3 agent wallets, CDP operations, and payment-capable workflows.
candidates.what_stark_must_do_better: Separate docs/context MCP from wallet/action MCP and require confirmation for transfers, swaps, payments, and key-sensitive operations.

candidates.name: QuickNode MCP
candidates.route_role: Web3 infrastructure route for endpoint inventory, creation, and security-rule management.
candidates.what_stark_must_do_better: Keep infrastructure admin separate from market analysis and require explicit confirmation before endpoint or security-rule changes.

candidates.name: CoinGecko MCP / Skill
candidates.route_role: Crypto market-data and token metadata route, especially when a neutral market-data source is needed.
candidates.what_stark_must_do_better: Resolve token IDs/contracts carefully and cross-check venue liquidity before turning market data into a thesis.

candidates.name: CoinMarketCap MCP
candidates.route_role: Crypto market-rank and neutral price context route alongside CoinGecko and exchange data.
candidates.what_stark_must_do_better: Resolve assets by ID/contract when possible and avoid treating rank or momentum as a direct trade signal.

candidates.name: Token Terminal MCP
candidates.route_role: Protocol-fundamentals route for DeFi/crypto DD, investable narratives, and sector comparisons.
candidates.what_stark_must_do_better: Cross-check methodology with Dune/DeFiLlama and keep financial metrics separate from trade recommendations.

candidates.name: DeFiLlama API
candidates.route_role: DeFi market-structure, protocol, yield, TVL, and fees/revenue evidence layer.
candidates.what_stark_must_do_better: Label methodology gaps and avoid using aggregate TVL/yield as a direct safety or trade signal.

candidates.name: Helius MCP
candidates.route_role: Solana/pump.fun-adjacent route for token, transaction, wallet, and launch-flow evidence.
candidates.what_stark_must_do_better: Separate Solana reads from transactions, cross-check token identity, and pair launch-flow metrics with liquidity and holder evidence.

candidates.name: Jupiter APIs
candidates.route_role: Solana quote/liquidity route for execution prep and market structure checks.
candidates.what_stark_must_do_better: Treat quotes as prep only until wallet, slippage, route, token address, and explicit confirmation are present.

candidates.name: DexScreener API
candidates.route_role: DEX market-display cross-check for liquidity, pair identity, and token market behavior.
candidates.what_stark_must_do_better: Use display data as one evidence layer only and treat community MCP wrappers as non-official until verified.

candidates.name: The Graph Subgraph MCP
candidates.route_role: Subgraph and indexed protocol-data route when a task needs schema-aware historical or protocol-specific evidence.
candidates.what_stark_must_do_better: Label schema freshness, index lag, and protocol-specific assumptions before turning indexed data into conclusions.

candidates.name: Goldsky MCP / AI Skills
candidates.route_role: Web3 data-pipeline route for Goldsky docs, subgraphs, streaming, and infrastructure context.
candidates.what_stark_must_do_better: Separate data-pipeline design from token-risk or trading conclusions and verify product surface, entitlement, and freshness.

candidates.name: Moralis Cortex MCP
candidates.route_role: Multichain Web3 data route for wallet, token, NFT, and transaction context.
candidates.what_stark_must_do_better: Resolve token contracts and chain identity, label freshness, and cross-check with explorer or chain-state sources.

candidates.name: GoldRush MCP
candidates.route_role: Multichain data cross-check route for balances, transactions, NFTs, and token price context.
candidates.what_stark_must_do_better: Treat portfolio and price data as evidence only, not as a direct risk label or trade recommendation.

candidates.name: SQD Portal MCP
candidates.route_role: Indexed multichain data route for logs, transactions, datasets, and high-volume chain analysis.
candidates.what_stark_must_do_better: Preserve dataset, chain, query-window, and indexing assumptions before using results in DD or trading analysis.

candidates.name: Stripe MCP / Agent Toolkit
candidates.route_role: Payments and revenue-ops route when a finance workflow touches billing, checkout, treasury, or x402/payment context.
candidates.what_stark_must_do_better: Keep payments separate from market signals and require explicit confirmation for state-changing payment or billing actions.

candidates.name: Plaid API
candidates.route_role: Financial account-data route for cashflow, treasury, and risk context outside brokerage execution.
candidates.what_stark_must_do_better: Treat account data as sensitive, avoid over-broad collection, and never confuse bank connectivity with broker trade execution.

candidates.name: Databento API
candidates.route_role: High-grade data candidate for serious strategy research when direct MCP is unavailable or unnecessary.
candidates.what_stark_must_do_better: Treat it as a data backbone candidate; require dataset, venue, entitlement, latency, and cost clarity.

candidates.name: IBKR TWS API / community MCP candidates
candidates.route_role: Broker API route for advanced users only after local TWS/Gateway, account, paper/live, and permissions are explicit.
candidates.what_stark_must_do_better: Do not treat community wrappers as official; require local environment, paper-mode proof, order preview, and kill-switch checks.

candidates.name: Hummingbot
candidates.route_role: Market-making and bot configuration review before sandbox or live deployment.
candidates.what_stark_must_do_better: Require inventory, rate-limit, cancel-failure, stale-data, max-loss, and kill-switch review.

candidates.name: Freqtrade
candidates.route_role: Crypto strategy testing, dry-run execution, and bot risk review.
candidates.what_stark_must_do_better: Reject decision-grade claims without fees, slippage, sizing, drawdown, and live bot guardrails.

candidates.name: LEAN Engine
candidates.route_role: Reproducible quant engine target for serious strategy workflows.
candidates.what_stark_must_do_better: Translate natural-language hypotheses into audited backtest specs and risk gates.

candidates.name: NautilusTrader
candidates.route_role: Advanced quant/backtest/live architecture candidate when strategy engineering needs production-grade event loops.
candidates.what_stark_must_do_better: Convert natural-language strategy requests into explicit data, venue, clock, risk, and deployment specs before touching live adapters.

candidates.name: CCXT
candidates.route_role: Exchange connector substrate for custom crypto data/trading adapters when vendor MCPs are missing.
candidates.what_stark_must_do_better: Treat library access as low-level power, not a safe agent surface; enforce rate limits, order preview, balances, and exchange-specific failure handling.
